message:
bench(e2e): add mt-read-bench for multi-thread SELECT-by-rowid reads

Seeds a fresh file-backed DB with N rows then spawns T threads each
running M prepared `SELECT payload FROM bench WHERE id = ?1` probes on
random rowids, one Connection per thread. Mirrors mt-mvcc-bench's
write-side shape so the read-side can be validated under the same
per-thread-Connection + BEGIN-CONCURRENT setup.

Run on HEAD (post-d98e9e31), release build, 5 000 rows, 20 000
reads/thread:

  threads | fs_rps  | sq_rps  | ratio
        1 |  18,931 | 288,428 | 0.07x
        2 |  49,392 | 384,124 | 0.13x
        4 |  95,721 | 440,139 | 0.22x
        8 | 128,548 | 422,902 | 0.30x

Findings (attached to bd-eyh2e as a bead comment):

- 6.8x MT scaling 1 -> 8t on the fsqlite side — the pinned-read wins
  d9c410bb + 7e4a5409 are paying off. fs/sq ratio climbs from 0.07x
  to 0.30x as threads scale because sqlite WAL-read contention caps
  around 2-4 threads.

- 15x gap on 1t SELECT-by-rowid latency (~53 µs per read vs ~3.5 µs
  for sqlite). This is NOT in the pager-read layer; it's elsewhere in
  parse/plan/prepare-cache/VDBE-dispatch/column-decode. Next levers
  surfaced: SimpleProjectedRowidLookup fast-path coverage (b86cd4e6),
  VDBE dispatch for PK-equality SELECT, column-decode allocation.

Bead:   bd-eyh2e (findings + reproduction attached as comment)
Usage:  cargo run --release -p fsqlite-e2e --bin mt-read-bench -- \
            [--rows=10000] [--reads-per-thread=50000] \
            [--threads=1,2,4,8]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1777,6 +1777,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-eorms","title":"Track F: Lock-Free Page Cache — Eliminate 0.25-0.40 µs Pager Lock Overhead","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:23:12.934512784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:27:18.919892911Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eorms","depends_on_id":"bd-e6hvd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:51:11.816393107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-eorms","depends_on_id":"bd-fzr07","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:51:11.253397261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3362,"issue_id":"bd-eorms","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Problem
Every page read acquires an RwLock on the pager's inner state + does a DashMap lookup. For a 3-level B-tree INSERT, that's 3 lock acquisitions = 150-450 ns of pure lock overhead. C SQLite has ZERO locks — pages are statically allocated pointers in a flat array.

## Solution: Thread-Local Page Pin Table (MICA Pattern from alien-graveyard)
Replace the locked DashMap page cache with a flat atomic array for the first 64K pages (256MB at 4KB pages). Page lookup becomes `pages[pgno]` — O(1), no hashing, no locking.

For single-connection mode (the common case): bypass ALL locking. The Connection already knows it's the only accessor. Store pages in a Vec<Option<PageData>> indexed by page number.

## Alien Graveyard Entries Referenced:
- §4.12 MICA Key-Value Store (flat array, no locking for single-thread)
- §3.8 Epoch-Based Reclamation (for safe page eviction under concurrent access)
- §2.1 Lock-Free Hash Tables (Herlihy-Shavit, for fallback on >64K pages)

## Expected Impact: -0.25 to -0.40 µs per INSERT row (the single largest win)

## Fallback: If the flat array causes memory pressure for large databases, fall back to the existing DashMap for pages > 64K. The hybrid approach costs zero for small databases and gracefully degrades.

## Proof Artifacts Required:
- Microbenchmark: page_lookup_latency_ns before/after
- Golden outputs: conformance_oracle_s55 must pass
- Memory usage: peak RSS before/after for 1K, 10K, 100K page databases
","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:23:38Z"}]}

Local diff was reduced to rustfmt comment-align drift from the already-shipped bench; stashed. Nothing new to ship for this lever.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-exher","title":"STUB-RSTUB-GEN-B-10: [NOT_IMPL] connection.rs:10220 (SURF-EXT-FTS5-015) — implement real tested perf-optimized code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:09:00.080763813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:17:41.876077123Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-exher","depends_on_id":"bd-bje80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:09:00.338247352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3598,"issue_id":"bd-exher","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Context
Stub ID: RSTUB-GEN-B-10 (tracked in runtime_stub_inventory.toml)
File: crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
Line: 10220
Feature: SURF-EXT-FTS5-015
Kind: not_implemented - Runtime guard returning FrankenError::NotImplemented. Entire code path is short-circuited and no behavior executes.
Severity: critical

## Code context (current stub)
```rust
    10213      /// Overwrites any existing function with the same `(name, num_args)` key.
    10214      pub fn register_scalar_function<F>(&self, function: F)
    10215      where
    10216          F: fsqlite_func::ScalarFunction + 'static,
    10217      {
    10218          let func_name = function.name().to_owned();
    10219          let func_args = function.num_args();
>>> 10220          let deterministic = function.is_deterministic();
    10221          tracing::info!(
    10222              target: "fsqlite.udf",
    10223              func_name = %func_name,
    10224              func_type = "scalar",
    10225              num_args = func_args,
    10226              deterministic = deterministic,
    10227              "udf_register"
    10228          );
```

## Target: real implementation
Feature scope: FTS5 full-text search feature - match SQLite FTS5 semantics for MATCH, tokenizers, rank functions, snippet/highlight, FTS5 auxiliary tables

Replace this stub with a correct, performance-optimized implementation that:
1. Matches SQLite reference semantics for the target feature (run against rusqlite oracle).
2. Respects the project's concurrent-mode-default=true invariant - do NOT serialize on file/page locks beyond what MVCC requires.
3. Uses asupersync primitives if async, never tokio.
4. Hot-path considerations: avoid heap allocation in the per-row loop, prefer SmallVec, inline-cache where identical patterns repeat, honor `hot_path_profile_enabled()` gate.
5. Passes `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at workspace level.

## Test coverage required
1. **Unit tests**: inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` - happy path, boundary (empty/max/NULL), error cases.
2. **Conformance oracle tests**: 5-10 tests in tests/conformance_oracle_*.rs comparing output vs rusqlite (C SQLite reference) on realistic query shapes.
3. **Property test** (when applicable): proptest for invariants under random valid inputs.
4. **E2E with structured logging**: when crossing subsystem boundaries (parser->planner->vdbe->storage), integration test in tests/ with tracing::debug! spans documenting inputs, intermediate states, outcome. Include trace_id, run_id, outcome.
5. **Regression coverage**: existing conformance oracle suites (s55, ext, s60-s84) MUST stay green.

## Performance guidance
- W5-6 hotspot table (bd-m4s2c/closed) identifies hot paths; use the profile if this stub is on one.
- PGO pipeline landed (W5-8 bd-njyvy/closed); PGO-instrumented rebuilds may amplify wins.
- SIMD integer-batch encode landed in W6-2 (bd-1sj4b/closed); similar column-dispatch patterns may apply.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Stub replaced with real implementation; `cargo check -p <crate>` clean
- [ ] Unit tests added and passing
- [ ] Conformance oracle tests added; rusqlite oracle matches
- [ ] E2E with logging added if boundary-crossing
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` clean
- [ ] Conformance oracle regression: s55, ext, s60-s84 still green
- [ ] runtime_stub_inventory.toml updated: mark this stub_id as resolved with date + committer

## Reference exemplars
- RSTUB-0010 (ATTACH/DETACH, resolved 2026-03-06 by BrightForge)
- RSTUB-0013 (REPLACE on non-IPK UNIQUE, resolved 2026-03-06 by BrightForge)
","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:17:41Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-eyh2e","title":"PERF-read: mt-read-bench shipped; 1t read path is 15x slower than SQLite","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:46:46.263942699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T21:46:55.919064336Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T21:46:55.918002689Z","close_reason":"mt-read-bench shipped. 1/2/4/8t: fs=18.9k/49.4k/95.7k/128.5k rps vs sq=288k/384k/440k/423k rps (ratio 0.07x/0.13x/0.22x/0.30x). Excellent 6.8x MT scaling across 1->8t (pinned-read wins d9c410bb+7e4a5409 are working) but 15x gap on 1t latency — dominated elsewhere, not in the pager read path. Next levers: re-check SimpleProjectedRowidLookup fast path coverage (b86cd4e6), VDBE dispatch for PK-equality SELECT, column-decode allocation cost. See bead comment for raw output + reproduction.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3684,"issue_id":"bd-eyh2e","author":"ubuntu","text":"# mt-read-bench — multi-threaded read-heavy findings (2026-04-24)

Shipped a new `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/mt_read_bench.rs` binary. Seeds
a fresh file-backed DB with N rows then spawns T threads each running M
prepared `SELECT payload FROM bench WHERE id = ?1` probes on random
rowids, one Connection per thread. Reports reads/sec and fs/sq ratio.

## Run 1 (HEAD post-d98e9e31, release build, local hardware)

```
mt-read-bench: rows=5000 reads_per_thread=20000 threads=[1, 2, 4, 8]
threads | fs_rps       | sq_rps       | ratio
      1 |        18931 |       288428 |  0.07x
      2 |        49392 |       384124 |  0.13x
      4 |        95721 |       440139 |  0.22x
      8 |       128548 |       422902 |  0.30x
```

## Observations

1. **1t read latency gap is the hot problem.** FrankenSQLite ~18.9k rps
   vs SQLite ~288k rps — 15x. Per-read wall clock ≈ 53 µs (fs) vs 3.5 µs
   (sq).

2. **fsqlite MT scaling is actually excellent.** 18.9k → 128.5k for
   1 → 8 threads = 6.8x speedup on 8 threads. Near-linear MT-read
   scaling, which is what the pinned-read path (d9c410bb, 7e4a5409)
   should buy.

3. **SQLite peaks between 2 and 4 threads**, then flat-lines around
   420-440k rps. Its WAL read contention bites earlier, so the fs/sq
   ratio climbs from 0.07x → 0.30x as threads scale. At 16+ threads the
   curves would likely cross in FrankenSQLite's favour — follow-up
   measurement opportunity.

4. **Single-thread is where the gap is.** Each SELECT touches
   parse+plan+prepare cache + VDBE dispatch + pager read + btree seek +
   column decode. Any of those layers adding ~50 µs of per-op overhead
   would explain the full gap. The pinned-read WAL optimisations
   (d9c410bb, 7e4a5409) touched the pager read layer; they clearly
   helped the MT scaling curve but the 1t latency floor is still
   dominated elsewhere.

## Suggested next levers (not shipped in this commit)

- **Prepared-statement reuse across reads**: verify the prepare cache
  is hitting for the mt-read-bench prepared stmt (the bench prepares
  once per connection, then binds + executes M times, so cache misses
  would imply a prepare-cache bug).

- **VDBE dispatch for SELECT-by-rowid hot path**: commit b86cd4e6
  (SimpleProjectedRowidLookup fast path for PK equality) targets this
  exact query shape. Re-run this bench with/without that path to
  quantify its 1t contribution.

- **Column-decode cost in the result row**: 64-byte TEXT payload
  decode plus the SqliteValue enum construction. If the result value
  is discarded (as in this bench), an evaluate-but-don't-materialize
  path could save the allocation.

- **Cursor reset between queries**: `query_with_params` may be
  constructing a fresh cursor each time instead of reusing one. Same
  for the MVCC transaction state (autocommit overhead per read).

## Artefacts

- `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/mt_read_bench.rs` (new binary)
- `crates/fsqlite-e2e/Cargo.toml` (bin registration)
- Raw output: `/tmp/mt-read-rpt/run1.txt`

## Run reproduction

```
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc3_btree_local cargo build --release \
    -p fsqlite-e2e --bin mt-read-bench
/tmp/rch_target_cc3_btree_local/release/mt-read-bench \
    --rows=5000 --reads-per-thread=20000 --threads=1,2,4,8
```
","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:46:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ezg4p","title":"TP: Track P unit + e2e tests — prefetch effectiveness, no invalid prefetch addresses","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:49:05.263515330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:27:06.514730175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ezg4p","depends_on_id":"bd-mb7gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:49:05.825732088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3407,"issue_id":"bd-ezg4p","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Unit Tests (crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs)
1. test_prefetch_valid_address — prefetch only issued for valid page numbers (no out-of-bounds)
2. test_prefetch_sequential_insert — sequential inserts prefetch rightmost child (100% hit rate)
3. test_prefetch_random_insert — random inserts prefetch midpoint child (~50% hit rate)
4. test_prefetch_no_crash_on_evicted — prefetch of recently-evicted page doesn't crash (just ignored)
5. test_cache_line_alignment — B-tree node headers are 64-byte aligned

## E2E Tests
6. test_prefetch_insert_10k_correctness — 10K inserts with prefetching, oracle comparison
7. test_prefetch_latency_improvement — compare p50/p95 descent latency with/without prefetch

## Logging
- prefetch_issued_count, prefetch_hit_count (estimated), descent_latency_ns per test
","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:49:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-f0w7y","title":"WASM 7.2: Interactive browser playground — SQL editor with syntax highlighting and live results","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:25:57.838380879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:26:25.269615163Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f0w7y","depends_on_id":"bd-2j5qu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-f0w7y","depends_on_id":"bd-xgz5z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1749,"issue_id":"bd-f0w7y","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Task 7.2: Interactive Browser Playground

## What
An interactive web-based SQL playground that runs FrankenSQLite WASM in the browser. Users can write SQL, see results, and explore the database — all client-side with zero server.

## Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ FrankenSQLite Playground                        │
├─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┤
│ SQL Editor              │ Results               │
│ (CodeMirror w/ SQL)     │ ┌─────────────────┐  │
│                         │ │ id│name │email   │  │
│ SELECT * FROM users     │ │ 1 │Alice│a@...   │  │
│ WHERE active = 1;       │ │ 2 │Bob  │b@...   │  │
│                         │ └─────────────────┘  │
│ [Run] [Clear] [Share]   │                       │
├─────────────────────────┤ Schema Browser        │
│ Examples                │ ├── users             │
│ • Create Table          │ │   ├── id (INTEGER)  │
│ • Insert Data           │ │   ├── name (TEXT)    │
│ • Complex Queries       │ │   └── email (TEXT)   │
│ • MVCC Demo             │ └── orders            │
└─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘
```

## Features
- CodeMirror editor with SQL syntax highlighting and autocomplete
- Real-time query results as table
- Schema browser showing tables, columns, indexes
- Share button (encode DB state in URL or generate share link)
- Pre-loaded examples showcasing FrankenSQLite features
- MVCC demo: show concurrent writers in action
- Export database as .sqlite file download

## Tech Stack
- React + Vite (or Next.js)
- CodeMirror 6 with SQL language mode
- @frankensqlite/react hooks for database interaction
- Tailwind CSS for styling

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads in <3 seconds on 3G network
- SQL execution with results display in <100ms
- Works offline after initial load
- Share URL encodes database state
- MVCC demo shows concurrent writers visually
","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:26:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-f6pn","title":"emit_expr ignores column refs in table-backed WHERE clauses (emits NULL)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:22:01.182645742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:39:22.937584324Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:35:32.827134469Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit c664162: ScanCtx threading through emit_expr for cursor-aware expression codegen. All WHERE clause column references now correctly emit Opcode::Column instead of Null.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1750,"issue_id":"bd-f6pn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Bug: emit_expr ignores column references in table-backed WHERE clauses

**File:** `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs`, line 1624-1627

**Root cause:** `emit_expr()` does not handle `Expr::Column` — it falls through to the
catch-all arm which emits `Opcode::Null`. This means any WHERE clause expression
involving column references (other than the specially-handled `Eq` case in
`emit_where_filter`) evaluates columns as NULL, breaking:
- Comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=, !=)
- OR predicates
- IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
- LIKE patterns
- BETWEEN / IN operators
- Any compound predicate that isn't a simple `col = literal`

**Symptoms:**
- `SELECT a FROM t WHERE a > 2` returns 0 rows
- `SELECT a FROM t WHERE a = 1 OR a = 3` returns 0 rows
- `SELECT a FROM t WHERE b IS NULL` returns ALL rows (NULL IS NULL = true)
- `SELECT b FROM t WHERE b LIKE 't%'` returns 0 rows

**Fix:** Add cursor-aware expression emission. Either:
1. Add `emit_expr_with_cursor(b, expr, reg, cursor, table)` that handles
   `Expr::Column` by emitting `Opcode::Column(cursor, col_idx, reg)`, OR
2. Refactor `emit_where_filter` to thread cursor context through to `emit_expr`

The `emit_where_filter` fallback (line 1366-1372) correctly evaluates the expression
and tests truthiness with `IfNot` — the only issue is that `emit_expr` can't resolve
column references.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:22:26Z"},{"id":1751,"issue_id":"bd-f6pn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Resolution: bd-f6pn — emit_expr column reference fix verified

The core fix was already implemented in commit c664162 ("feat(vdbe): thread ScanCtx through emit_expr for cursor-aware expression codegen"):

1. ScanCtx struct carries cursor + table context through expression emission
2. emit_expr handles Expr::Column when ScanCtx is provided, emitting Opcode::Column
3. emit_where_filter catch-all creates ScanCtx and passes it for non-Eq/non-And WHERE predicates
4. emit_column_reads creates ScanCtx for non-column expressions in SELECT list
5. All helpers (emit_binary_op, emit_comparison, emit_is_comparison, emit_case_expr) propagate ctx

What I fixed: Updated outdated test test_codegen_select_non_column_expr_with_from_rejected to
test_codegen_select_non_column_expr_with_from_accepted since emit_column_reads now handles
non-column expressions via ScanCtx. Test verifies Add opcode is emitted for SELECT 1+2 FROM t.

Verification: 168 VDBE tests pass, 64 fsqlite lib tests pass, clippy+fmt clean.
All symptoms from the bug report are resolved.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:39:22Z"}]}
@@ -1840,6 +1841,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-jd39","title":"5F.2: End-to-end storage stack test script with structured logging","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:22:14.571573581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:25:08.720880090Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:25:08.720855023Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jd39","depends_on_id":"bd-1xrs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jd39","depends_on_id":"bd-2mgc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1795,"issue_id":"bd-jd39","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# 5F.2: End-to-end storage stack test script with structured logging

## What

A comprehensive integration test that exercises the ENTIRE Phase 5 pipeline from database
creation to concurrent writers, with detailed structured logging at every step. This is the
definitive "does the storage stack work?" test.

## Test structure

The test follows a real-world database lifecycle:

### Stage 1: Database creation and DDL (validates 5A)
```
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=create_db] Creating fresh database at {path}
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=create_table] CREATE TABLE users(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE)
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=verify_sqlite_master] sqlite_master entries=1 root_page=2
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=create_index] CREATE INDEX idx_email ON users(email)
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=verify_sqlite_master] sqlite_master entries=2 idx_root_page=3
[E2E][stage=1_ddl][step=schema_cookie] cookie_value=2 (incremented twice)
```

### Stage 2: Write operations (validates 5B)
```
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=insert_batch] Inserting 1000 rows via autocommit
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=insert_progress] inserted=100/1000 elapsed_ms=12
...
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=insert_complete] inserted=1000 total_ms=87 rows_per_sec=11494
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=update_batch] Updating 500 rows
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=delete_batch] Deleting 100 rows
[E2E][stage=2_write][step=verify_counts] expected=900 actual=900
```

### Stage 3: Read operations (validates 5C)
```
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=full_scan] SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=full_scan_result] rows=900 elapsed_ms=5
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=index_seek] SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'user500@test.com'
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=index_seek_result] rows=1 elapsed_ms=0
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=aggregate] SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(id) FROM users
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=aggregate_result] count=900 sum=expected_sum
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=join] SELECT u.name FROM users u WHERE u.id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE id < 10)
[E2E][stage=3_read][step=join_result] rows=9
```

### Stage 4: Transaction lifecycle (validates 5D)
```
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=begin] BEGIN
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=insert] INSERT INTO users VALUES (10001, 'txn_user', 'txn@test.com')
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=rollback] ROLLBACK
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=verify_rollback] SELECT WHERE id=10001 → rows=0 ✓
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=begin] BEGIN
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=insert] INSERT INTO users VALUES (10001, 'txn_user', 'txn@test.com')
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=savepoint] SAVEPOINT sp1
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=insert] INSERT INTO users VALUES (10002, 'sp_user', 'sp@test.com')
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=rollback_to] ROLLBACK TO sp1
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=verify] id=10001 present ✓, id=10002 absent ✓
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=commit] COMMIT
[E2E][stage=4_txn][step=verify_commit] id=10001 present ✓
```

### Stage 5: Persistence and reopen (validates 5D + WAL)
```
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=checkpoint] PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=checkpoint_result] pages_checkpointed=N
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=close] Connection::close()
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=reopen] Connection::open({path})
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=verify] SELECT COUNT(*) = 901 ✓
[E2E][stage=5_persist][step=verify_data] Spot-checking 10 random rows... all match ✓
```

### Stage 6: Concurrent writers (validates 5E)
```
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=setup] Creating accounts table with 100 rows, balance=1000 each
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=spawn] Spawning 4 concurrent writer threads
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=thread_0] commits=250 retries=12 restarts=3 elapsed_ms=340
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=thread_1] commits=250 retries=8 restarts=5 elapsed_ms=360
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=thread_2] commits=250 retries=15 restarts=1 elapsed_ms=310
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=thread_3] commits=250 retries=10 restarts=4 elapsed_ms=350
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=verify_conservation] SUM(balance)=100000 ✓ (expected=100000)
[E2E][stage=6_mvcc][step=verify_no_locks] Lock table empty ✓
```

### Stage 7: Final integrity check
```
[E2E][stage=7_integrity][step=pragma] PRAGMA integrity_check
[E2E][stage=7_integrity][step=result] ok ✓
[E2E][stage=7_integrity][step=page_count] total_pages=N
[E2E][stage=7_integrity][step=freelist] freelist_pages=M
[E2E][stage=7_integrity][step=summary] ALL 7 STAGES PASSED | total_elapsed_ms=1234
```

## Implementation

This test lives in `tests/e2e_storage_stack.rs` as an integration test.

Key requirements:
- Uses `tempdir()` for isolated test databases
- Each stage is a separate function that returns `Result<StageReport>`
- `StageReport` contains timing, assertion results, and structured log entries
- On any failure, dump full diagnostic state before panicking
- Print final summary with timing breakdown per stage
- Can be run standalone: `cargo test --test e2e_storage_stack -- --nocapture`

## Diagnostic dump on failure

When any assertion fails, before panicking:
```rust
fn dump_diagnostics(conn: &Connection, path: &str) {
    eprintln!("[DIAG] Database path: {path}");
    eprintln!("[DIAG] Page size: {}", conn.pragma_query_value("page_size"));
    eprintln!("[DIAG] Page count: {}", conn.pragma_query_value("page_count"));
    eprintln!("[DIAG] WAL file size: {} bytes", fs::metadata(format!("{path}-wal")).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0));
    eprintln!("[DIAG] Journal mode: {}", conn.pragma_query_value("journal_mode"));
    eprintln!("[DIAG] Integrity check: {}", conn.pragma_query_value("integrity_check"));
    // Dump sqlite_master
    for row in conn.query("SELECT * FROM sqlite_master") {
        eprintln!("[DIAG] sqlite_master: {row:?}");
    }
}
```

## Tests

- The e2e script IS the test — it's self-verifying at each stage
- `test_e2e_storage_stack_full`: Run all 7 stages end-to-end
- `test_e2e_storage_stack_stage_N`: Individual stages for targeted debugging

 {"id":"bd-jfsi2","title":"V-TEST-2: Unit + e2e test suite for V2 superinstruction fusion + ceremony reduction","description":"## What
Write tests that verify superinstruction correctness and per-opcode ceremony changes.

## Test categories

### V2.1 FusedAppendInsert tests
- `test_fused_append_insert_simple_1col`: INSERT INTO t(x) VALUES(?1) with 100 rows, verify all rows present and correct
- `test_fused_append_insert_multicol`: INSERT INTO t(a,b,c) VALUES(?1,?2,?3) with varied types (int, text, float, null, blob)
- `test_fused_append_insert_autoincrement`: table with AUTOINCREMENT, verify rowids are monotonically increasing and sqlite_sequence updated
- `test_fused_append_insert_empty_table`: first INSERT into empty table (no cached last_alloc_rowid), verify correctness
- `test_fused_append_insert_after_delete`: INSERT after DELETE (gaps in rowids), verify no rowid reuse if AUTOINCREMENT
- `test_fused_insert_NOT_fused_with_index`: table with secondary index, verify fused instruction is NOT generated (pattern guard)
- `test_fused_insert_NOT_fused_with_trigger`: table with BEFORE INSERT trigger, verify normal opcodes used
- `test_fused_insert_NOT_fused_with_conflict`: INSERT OR REPLACE, verify normal opcodes used
- `test_fused_insert_NOT_fused_with_fk`: table with foreign key, verify normal opcodes used
- `test_fused_insert_explain_output`: EXPLAIN of simple INSERT shows FusedAppendInsert opcode
- `test_fused_insert_explain_complex_shows_normal`: EXPLAIN of INSERT with index shows normal NewRowid+MakeRecord+Insert

### V2.2 FusedOpenWriteLast tests  
- `test_fused_open_write_last_simple`: verify cursor opens and positions at end
- `test_fused_open_write_last_empty_table`: empty table, verify cursor at EOF
- `test_fused_open_write_last_with_existing_data`: table with rows, verify cursor at last row

### V2.3 Ceremony reduction tests
- `test_sentinel_halt_terminates_loop`: program with no explicit Halt, verify sentinel added by codegen
- `test_metrics_disabled_in_release`: verify opcode_count is not incremented when feature disabled
- `test_cancellation_still_works`: verify long-running query can be cancelled even without bounds check

### E2E regression tests
- `test_e2e_fused_insert_10k_correctness`: 10K row INSERT via fused path, SELECT all rows, verify content + order
- `test_e2e_fused_insert_matches_interpreter`: run same INSERT via fused path and normal path (force fallback), compare all output (rowids, values, changes count, last_insert_rowid)
- `test_e2e_fused_insert_page_split_correctness`: INSERT enough rows to trigger multiple B-tree page splits, verify integrity_check passes
- `test_e2e_mixed_fused_and_normal`: mix of fused INSERTs and non-fused UPDATEs in same transaction, verify correctness

### Logging
- Log whether each INSERT used fused or normal path (add counter: FSQLITE_FUSED_APPEND_INSERT_COUNT)
- Log serialized record size for fused path
- Log page splits during fused inserts
- Log B-tree depth changes

## Files
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/tests/v2_superinstruction_tests.rs` (NEW)
- `crates/fsqlite-core/tests/v2_e2e_fused_insert_tests.rs` (NEW)

## Verification
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe --test v2_superinstruction_tests`
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-core --test v2_e2e_fused_insert_tests`
- Conformance oracle suite (ext + s55) passes
- Benchmark section 1: verify improvement with fused path enabled vs disabled

 {"id":"bd-jwuo","title":"Fixture selection UX: tags/filters + stable db_id resolution","description":"## Goal

Make fixture selection ergonomic and unambiguous for humans and automation.

## Requirements

- Every fixture has a stable `db_id` (manifest-driven) that is used across:
  - CLI flags (`--db <db_id>`)
  - JSON reports
  - TUI UI

- Add filtering by:
  - tag (`--tag beads`)
  - size range
  - requires header_ok
  - has_wal_sidecar_at_capture

- Resolve ambiguity:
  - if user passes a stem that matches multiple, show all candidates and fail with a clear error.

## Acceptance

- `realdb-e2e run --db <id>` never silently picks the wrong DB.
- `realdb-e2e corpus scan` output aligns with the IDs the rest of the harness uses.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance Criteria

1. Stable db_id resolution:
   - CLI commands (`realdb-e2e run`, `bench`, `corrupt`, `verify`) accept `--db <db_id>` and resolve via the corpus manifest/metadata.
   - If a user-provided identifier is ambiguous, the CLI prints all candidates and exits non-zero (no silent choice).

2. Filtering UX:
   - Support filtering fixture selection by at least:
     - tag (taxonomy defined in `bd-1n0i`)
     - size range or size bucket
     - header_ok
     - captured-with-wal-sidecar

3. Reporting contract:
   - All JSON/JSONL outputs emitted by the harness use `db_id` (not a path) as the stable fixture identifier.

4. Tests:
   - Add tests that validate ambiguous resolution behavior and tag filters.","notes":"Notes

- This bead turns the corpus into a *user-facing interface*: stable identifiers, reproducible selection, and trustworthy reports.
- Prefer resolving by manifest entry, not by scanning the filesystem at run time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:25.908275920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:35.095488915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:10:01.745205828Z","close_reason":"fixture_select module complete: manifest loading, exact/substring resolution, ambiguity detection, tag/size/feature filtering, 21 tests passing, clippy+fmt clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jwuo","depends_on_id":"bd-1n0i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jwuo","depends_on_id":"bd-2als.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jwuo","depends_on_id":"bd-5ic3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1798,"issue_id":"bd-jwuo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-jwuo Fixture Selection UX — Complete

### Summary
New `fixture_select` module provides ergonomic, unambiguous fixture resolution with tag/size/feature filtering. Loads manifest.v1.json, resolves db_ids with ambiguity detection, and provides CLI argument parsing. All 21 tests pass including real manifest validation. Clippy (pedantic+nursery) clean, fmt clean.

### Module: `fixture_select.rs` (~600 lines)

**Core Types:**
- `ManifestEntry` — struct for corpus manifest entries (db_id, golden_filename, sha256_golden, size_bytes, tags, sqlite_meta)
- `Manifest` — top-level manifest file structure
- `FixtureFilter` — filter criteria: db_selector, require_tags, exclude_tags, min/max_size_bytes, requires_wal, ci_safe_only
- `SelectionResult` enum — Single(entry), Ambiguous{selector, candidates}, NoMatch{reason}

**Functions:**
- `load_manifest()` / `load_manifest_from()` — load manifest.v1.json
- `select()` — resolve fixture with exact→substring fallback + secondary filters
- `select_all()` — return all matching fixtures as vec
- `parse_filter_args()` — CLI argument parser for --db, --tag, --exclude-tag, --min-size, --max-size, --requires-wal, --ci-safe
- `parse_size()` — parse human-readable sizes (K/M/G suffixes)
- `format_size()` — format bytes as human-readable
- `format_fixture_table()` — format entries as aligned text table
- `resolve_golden_path()` / `resolve_metadata_path()` — path resolution helpers

**Ambiguity Detection:**
- Exact match on db_id takes priority over substring
- Multiple substring matches → Ambiguous result with all candidates listed
- `require_single()` produces clear error: lists all candidates with size info

**CLI Flags:**
- `--db <ID>` — select by exact or substring match
- `--tag <TAG>` — require tag (repeatable)
- `--exclude-tag <TAG>` — exclude tag
- `--min-size <SIZE>` / `--max-size <SIZE>` — size range (supports K/M/G)
- `--requires-wal` — only WAL-mode fixtures
- `--ci-safe` — only CI-safe fixtures

### Tests (21 total)
1-6: Selection logic (exact, substring, ambiguous, no-match, require_single ok/error)
7-11: Secondary filters (tags, exclude tags, size range, WAL, combined)
12-14: Size parsing, formatting, filter arg parsing
15-17: Fixture table, path resolution, help text
18: Real manifest loading & validation
19-21: select_all, no-match description, empty filter
","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:09:56Z"},{"id":1799,"issue_id":"bd-jwuo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-jwuo Completion: Fixture selection UX

### Already Implemented (fixture_select.rs, 938 lines)
- Stable db_id resolution: exact match (priority 1), substring match (priority 2)
- Ambiguity detection: multiple matches listed with filenames and sizes
- Tag filtering: --tag TAG (require ALL), --exclude-tag TAG (exclude ANY)
- Size range filtering: --min-size SIZE, --max-size SIZE (K/M/G suffixes)
- WAL mode filtering: --requires-wal
- CLI arg parsing, manifest loading, display formatting

### New Additions (this session)
1. sync_tags_from_metadata() - reads metadata JSON and populates tags in manifest. 34/34 updated.
2. save_manifest() - writes manifest to disk as pretty-printed JSON.
3. find_duplicate_db_ids() - validates db_id uniqueness.
4. Updated manifest.v1.json - all 34 entries now have tags populated from metadata.
5. 4 new tests for uniqueness validation and tag sync.

### Acceptance Criteria Met
- realdb-e2e run --db never silently picks wrong DB (ambiguity detection)
- All db_ids unique (validated by test)
- Tag filtering works end-to-end (tags in manifest)

### Validation: 495 e2e tests passing, clippy + fmt clean

 {"id":"bd-jxvns","title":"EPIC: Benchmark Tooling & Build Health (Track A remainder)","description":"## What

Remaining benchmark tooling, metadata, and build health items from the performance program. The major benchmark fairness fixes (prepared statements, honest naming, README docs) are already done. This epic covers the remaining ceremony that would be nice but should NOT block Track C or Track B work.

## Items
- Explicit per-benchmark metadata annotations (prepared/ad-hoc/file/in-memory/concurrent)
- Benchmark gate that fails if SQLite and FrankenSQLite use different statement-lifecycle modes
- Top-level wrapper command for hot-profile invocation
- CI default hot-path summary markdown
- Comparison artifact (SQLite vs FrankenSQLite component costs side by side)
- Fix pre-existing build blockers (fmt, check, clippy)

## Priority

P2. These are SUPPORT tasks. They make the development experience better but they do NOT move the concurrent-writer needle. Track C moves the needle.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:23.399258224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:23.399258224Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","tooling","track-a"]}
 {"id":"bd-jxvns.1","title":"A1: Fix pre-existing build blockers (fmt, check, clippy)","description":"## What

Three pre-existing build issues prevent clean workspace-wide verification:

1. cargo fmt --check: Formatting drift in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs and crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
2. cargo check --workspace --all-targets: Borrow-check failures in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
3. cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: Failures in crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/physical_merge.rs

## Why P1

These don't block Track C design work (C1, C2), but they DO block verification of any implementation work. Should be fixed before C3 implementation starts but not before C1/C2 design.

## Approach
- Fix each file individually
- Run targeted checks after each fix
- Do NOT attempt workspace-wide cargo check until all three are fixed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:33.311607664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T21:44:29.814228230Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T21:44:29.813863707Z","close_reason":"All three build blockers resolved: (1) cargo fmt --all --check passes, (2) cargo check --workspace --all-targets passes, (3) cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes with only 1 acceptable warning in test code (too_many_arguments). Fixes applied: pager.rs #[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)], physical_merge.rs elided lifetimes, cell_visibility.rs #[allow(clippy::missing_fields_in_debug)] + u64::from cast.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","hygiene","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jxvns.1","depends_on_id":"bd-jxvns","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:33.311607664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
